3 min read
1 hour ago

--

AI has completely changed the way we build software.
Today, tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, and Lovable can generate impressive React components from a simple prompt. What once took hours can now be done in seconds.
But after the code is generated, a surprisingly old problem remains.
How do you quickly see the result?
The Workflow Nobody Enjoys
Imagine you receive a JSX component from an AI assistant.
You don’t want to edit it yet.
You just want to know one thing:
“What does it look like?”
Instead of getting an instant answer, the usual workflow looks like this:

• Create a React project (or find an existing one)

• Copy the JSX file

• Install missing packages

• Fix import errors

• Run the development server

• Wait for everything to compile
Only then can you preview a single component.
It’s a workflow we’ve accepted for years — but it doesn’t have to stay that way.
The Inspiration Behind PreviewKit
I found myself repeating this process almost every day.
Sometimes I wanted to compare multiple AI-generated UI ideas.
Sometimes I only needed to verify a layout before moving on.
Each time, I had to go through the same setup process.
Eventually, I asked myself a simple question:
Why can’t a JSX file open as easily as an image or a PDF?
That question became the foundation of PreviewKit.
A Different Way to Preview JSX
PreviewKit is a lightweight Windows application built for one purpose:
Open a JSX file and preview it instantly.
No React project.
No dependency installation.
No unnecessary setup.
Just open the file and see the component.
That’s it.
Built for the AI Development Era
The rise of AI coding assistants has dramatically increased the number of components developers generate every day.
Generating code is now incredibly fast.
Reviewing it should be just as fast.
PreviewKit fits naturally into this new workflow by reducing the time between code generation and visual feedback.
Whether your component comes from ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Bolt, Lovable, or your own editor, the experience should be the same:
Open. Preview. Continue building.
Who Can Benefit?
PreviewKit is useful for anyone who works with React components:

• Frontend Developers

• React Engineers

• UI Designers

• Students learning React

• Freelancers

• AI-assisted developers

• Teams reviewing UI components
If JSX files are part of your daily workflow, PreviewKit can help eliminate repetitive setup work.
The Smallest Improvements Often Matter the Most
Not every productivity improvement needs to save hours.
Sometimes saving a minute — or even a few seconds — on a repetitive task adds up over weeks and months.
PreviewKit focuses on removing one of those small but frustrating interruptions.
Because building software should be about creating great experiences, not repeatedly setting up the same environment.
Final Thoughts
AI has accelerated software development in incredible ways.
Now it’s time for the surrounding tools to catch up.
PreviewKit isn’t trying to replace React or your favorite development environment.
It’s simply designed to make one common task faster:
Previewing JSX files.
Less setup.
More building.
